channel.title: La Cyber Sphère — Bulletin de veille cyber
channel.description: Alertes critiques et élevées, résumées et sourcées.

item.title: [Faible] Microsoft shares manual fix for WSUS sync delays and timeouts
item.description: Microsoft has shared manual mitigations to help IT administrators fix Windows Server Update Services (WSUS) servers affected by a known issue that causes Windows Update scans to fail or time out. [...]

item.title: [Critique] WordPress wp2shell Exploitation Grows as Public Exploit Fuels Mass Scanning
item.description:
Attackers have begun to exploit two critical vulnerabilities in WordPress that, when combined together, enable unauthenticated remote code execution (RCE) and complete compromise of vulnerable websites.
The two…

item.title: [Faible] Fuite revendiquée au Rassemblement national ?
item.description: Un pirate affirme vendre des données présumés volés au Rassemblement national qui révélerait des données internes, sans preuve publique permettant d’authentifier la fuite à ce stade.

item.title: [Faible] Hugging Face frappé par un agent cyber autonome
item.description: Une attaque autonome contre Hugging Face révèle les risques cyber des pipelines de données et des agents IA.

item.title: [Critique] Windows LegacyHive zero-day flaw gets free, unofficial patches
item.description: Free unofficial patches are available for a recently disclosed Windows zero-day flaw that allows attackers to escalate privileges on up-to-date Windows systems. [...]

item.title: [Critique] New ENCFORGE Ransomware Targets AI Model Files in Langflow RCE Attack
item.description:
Researchers at Sysdig have linked a second attack on the same Langflow server to JADEPUFFER, the AI-agent-driven operator it first documented earlier this month.
The same operator has now been spotted deploying…

item.title: [Moyen] Critical ServiceNow AI Platform Flaw Exploited for Unauthenticated Code Execution
item.description:
Threat actors are now exploiting a recently disclosed critical security flaw impacting ServiceNow AI Platform, according to Defused Cyber.
In a post shared on X, the threat intelligence firm said it's observing…

item.title: [Critique] Estée Lauder discloses data breach via Oracle E-Business flaw
item.description: Cosmetics giant Estée Lauder is notifying customers of a data breach after hackers exploited a flaw in Oracle E-Business Suite that the company used for human resources (HR) operations. [...]

item.title: [Critique] SonicWall SMA1000 flaws exploited as zero-days to push custom malware
item.description: Two recently disclosed SonicWall SMA1000 vulnerabilities were exploited in zero-day attacks for weeks, allowing threat actors to install custom malware on vulnerable VPN appliances. [...]

item.title: [Élevé] Hackers steal $23.7 million in crypto from Ostium in off-chain attack
item.description: The Ostium trading platform announced that an attacker stole $23.75 million from its liquidity provider vault last week, after compromising off-chain infrastructure used to feed prices into the protocol. [...]

item.title: [Moyen] Cursor, Codex, Gemini CLI, Antigravity hit by sandbox escapes
item.description: Researchers escaped the sandboxes in Cursor, Codex, Gemini CLI and Antigravity by having the AI agent write files that trusted host tools later run. Multiple CVEs, patches, and Google downgrading two Antigravity…

item.title: [Critique] JadePuffer agentic attacks now target AI model data with ransomware
item.description: The JadePuffer autonomous AI agent has upgraded with custom malware called EncForge that focuses on encrypting AI assets, such as training datasets, vector databases, and model checkpoints. [...]

item.title: [Faible] Flock Safety kills acoustic system designed to detect 'human distress'
item.description: "Community consultation" is one of the reasons automated license plate reader (ALPR) company Flock Safety cited in its decision to drop voice-oriented tech from a gunshot detection system.

item.title: [Faible] FakeGit Campaign Uses 7,600 GitHub Repositories to Spread SmartLoader Malware
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered nearly 7,600 malicious GitHub repositories, out of which more than 800 pose as artificial intelligence (AI) skills or Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers to deliver a malware…

item.title: [Élevé] India says allegedly leaked nuclear plant files pose no safety risk
item.description: Documents that the World Leaks cybercrime group claimed to leak from the Kudankulam Nuclear Power Plant do not contain information pertaining to safety or security, Indian officials said.

item.title: [Élevé] New HollowGraph malware uses Microsoft Graph for stealthy C2 comms
item.description: A malicious component dubbed HollowGraph uses the calendar feature in compromised Microsoft 365 mailboxes as a command-and-control channel to receive attacker commands and exfiltrate stolen data. [...]

item.title: [Faible] Fraude scolaire : le piège des frais de scolarité est de retour
item.description: Une fausse facture scolaire de 350 € cible des étudiants avec un virement urgent et une adresse trompeuse.

item.title: [Faible] Exposed Server Reveals AI-Assisted Phishing Toolkit Behind WebDAV Malware Campaign
item.description: A malware operator left its delivery server wide open, and Rapid7 pulled down the whole toolkit: 1,048 files spanning lure templates, filename-spoofing tests, execution experiments, droppers, builder notes, and two…

item.title: [Faible] More than 1,000 domains illegally streaming World Cup games seized, DOJ says
item.description: Over the course of the World Cup tournament, the Department of Justice seized more than 1,000 domains for illegally streaming games.

item.title: [Élevé] Hackers were inside South Korea's diplomat training system for 9 months
item.description: Unidentified hackers compromised an online education system used by South Korea's diplomatic academy, stealing personal information belonging to former and current employees of the country's Ministry of Foreign Affairs.

item.title: [Faible] France Casse : 3,3 millions de comptes en fuite avec des mots de passe en clair
item.description: Le site France Casse, plateforme française spécialisée dans la recherche et la vente de pièces automobiles d’occasion, fait... L’article France Casse : 3,3 millions de comptes en fuite avec des mots de passe en…

item.title: [Faible] Romania races to restore land registry after cyberattack disrupts property market
item.description: Romania's land registry agency is still recovering from a cyberattack it called "the most serious technical incident in the institution's history."

item.title: [Faible] HollowGraph Malware Hides C2 and Stolen Files in Microsoft 365 Events Dated 2050
item.description: A newly discovered espionage implant has been using a hijacked Microsoft 365 calendar as its command channel, planting operator instructions and smuggling out stolen files as attachments on calendar events dated to the…

item.title: [Critique] PSB Paris School of Business : 864 dossiers d’inscription d’étudiants mis en vente avec pièces d’identité
item.description: PSB Paris School of Business, école de commerce parisienne appartenant au groupe Galileo Global Education, fait l’objet d’une... L’article PSB Paris School of Business : 864 dossiers d’inscription…

item.title: [Faible] Centre Hospitalier de Perpignan : des dossiers de patients en fuite
item.description: Le Centre Hospitalier de Perpignan fait l’objet d’une revendication publiée le 19 juillet 2026 par un collectif se... L’article Centre Hospitalier de Perpignan : des dossiers de patients en fuite est apparu en premier…

item.title: [Faible] An AI SOC Evaluation Guide for Security Leaders
item.description: Choosing an AI SOC platform requires understanding how it will perform in your own environment, not just during an evaluation. Prophet Security shares a practical framework for assessing AI SOC solutions, including how…

item.title: [Critique] ⚡ Weekly Recap: WordPress RCE, SonicWall 0-Days, AI Service Attacks, SharePoint 0-Day and More
item.description:
A single request should not be able to do this much. But this week, small inputs led to code execution, memory loss, stolen keys, and disabled security tools.
The paths were often simple: exposed systems, weak checks,…

item.title: [Faible] Russian Intelligence Hacks IP Cameras to Spy on Military Logistics Across NATO States and Ukraine
item.description: At least one Russian intelligence service is systematically hijacking internet-connected security cameras across Europe and Ukraine, using the feeds to watch military transport routes, weapons shipments bound for Kyiv,…

item.title: [Faible] Hugging Face warns an autonomous AI agent hacked its network
item.description: The Hugging Face artificial intelligence repository disclosed that attackers gained access to internal datasets and credentials after breaching its production infrastructure using an autonomous AI agent system. [...]

item.title: [Faible] Mythos Didn't Break Your Security Program. Your Exposure Window Could.
item.description: The industry spent the initial months after Anthropic's April 7 Mythos reveal focused on volume. How many new CVEs would Mythos add to an already overloaded pipeline? How quickly would the flood of AI-driven discovery…

item.title: [Faible] Software provider to more than 2,000 US hospitals says hackers stole employee and customer data
item.description: Craneware, which is headquartered in Edinburgh and listed on London's AIM market, told investors it detected unauthorized access to a “subset” of its data environment and has since brought in outside forensic…

item.title: [Faible] Microsoft confirms Windows Server Update Services sync delays
item.description: Microsoft is working to fix a known issue affecting Windows Server Update Services (WSUS) servers, which has caused synchronization problems for more than a week. [...]

item.title: [Faible] Windows KB5121767 OOB update fixes shutdowns on some Dell PCs
item.description: Microsoft has released emergency updates to fix a known issue causing some Dell PCs to shut down after installing the July 2026 Windows 11 security updates. [...]
